relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Jesus continues teaching beyond the Jordan.
A divorce question arrives as a test.
Jesus returns them to Moses.
They cite the document of release.
Jesus names hardness of heart as the reason for concession.
He returns to creation’s beginning.
Joining requires leaving and cleaving.
The two become one flesh.
What the Divine joins should not be casually separated.
The disciples ask again in the house.
Jesus protects covenant from abandonment.
The teaching applies in both directions.
Children are brought for touch, but disciples block them.
Jesus is indignant and opens the way for children.
The reign must be received like a child.
He holds and blesses them.
A man runs to ask about age-long life.
Jesus redirects the word good toward the Divine.
The commands are named.
The man has guarded them from youth.
Jesus looks at him, loves him, and names the one lack.
Possessions hold him in grief.
Wealth makes entering the reign difficult.
The disciples are amazed at the difficulty.
The camel and needle image exposes wealth’s impossibility as security.
They ask who can be saved.
Impossible for humans is possible with the Divine.
Peter points to what they have left.
Leaving for Jesus and the good message is remembered.
The new family comes with pressures and age-long life in the coming age.
First and last are reversed.
On the way to Jerusalem, amazement and fear surround them.
Jesus names the handing-over to authorities and nations.
Mockery, death, and rising are foretold.
James and John ask for whatever they want.
Jesus asks what they desire.
They seek seats at right and left in glory.
Jesus asks whether they can drink the cup and share the baptism.
They will share the cup and baptism.
The seats belong to preparation beyond ambition.
The ten become indignant at the two.
Nation-power lords over others.
Greatness among disciples becomes service.
Firstness becomes slavery to all.
The Son of Humanity serves and gives his life as ransom for many.
Bartimaeus sits blind beside the way.
He cries for mercy from the Son of David.
The crowd tries to silence him, but mercy cries louder.
Jesus stops and calls him.
He throws off his cloak and comes.
He asks to see again.
Trust saves/heals him, and he follows on the way.
